summary: Trojan-loader wrapper pulls unused @sqlite-node/createsql dependency on require
details: The package presents itself as a SQL/SQLite helper (`@sql-access/nodesql`) but its README advertises an unrelated streaming library (`bare-stream`), and `index.js` is a verbatim copy of the feross/buffer Buffer polyfill. The first executable statement at index.js:10 is `var ins = require('@sqlite-node/createsql');`, where the variable `ins` is never referenced elsewhere in the file. Loading a scoped dependency solely for its import-time side effects, inside a file otherwise copied wholesale from an unrelated open-source project, is the canonical loader-wrapper shape: this package serves no functional purpose of its own and exists to silently pull `@sqlite-node/createsql` into the installer's dependency tree and execute its top-level code in the consumer process. The three-way mismatch between the package name (SQL access), the README (bare-stream), and the actual code (Buffer polyfill) is consistent with a deliberate lure rather than an honest mis-publish.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
